phase: S8 pre-flight (pre-S9), 2nd pass after concurrent CLAIM.md rewrite

claim_md_rewrite_observed.summary: Between Agent B's 1st pass and 2nd pass, docs/CLAIM.md was rewritten: (a) tag placement moved from bullet-leading to line-leading (resolves former B-M1 / C-B3); (b) line 11 OTCP claim softened from '100 bootstrap trials, 95%' to '... the bootstrap trials in tests/test_otcp.py::test_otcp_calibrate_marginal_coverage_synthetic_holds' (resolves former B-B1); (c) line 13 PAVA claim softened from 'strict copy + inherits foldconsensus's PAVA tests' to 'vendored from foldconsensus and inherits foldconsensus PAVA invariants' (resolves former B-M2 acceptably); (d) line 45 introduces a NEW [CLAIM] for a `random`/`planted` regime in the ablation runner — see B-B2 below.

blockers.title: docs/CLAIM.md:45 [CLAIM] tag asserts an UNSHIPPED feature (random/planted regime)
blockers.claim_text: [CLAIM] The ablation runner ships two synthetic regimes (`tests/test_ablation_runner.py` verifies both run): a `random` regime (random Gaussian decoders, no planted alignment, expected top-k ~ 0) and a `planted` regime (bundle B is bundle A's decoder permuted by a fixed-seed permutation; the synthetic ground truth points to that permutation). The `planted` regime returns non-trivial top-k > 0 on the same `[DEMO]` rows, and the JSON cell records `regime` and the planted permutation seed.
actual_state: `grep -nE 'regime|planted' scripts/run_ablation.py` rc=1 (no match — only one synthetic regime implemented, random Gaussian decoders at lines 53-61).
`grep -nE 'regime|planted' results/v0.1.0a1_ablation.json` rc=1 (no regime key in env_stamp or metrics_by_setting).
`grep -nE 'regime|planted' tests/test_ablation_runner.py` rc=1; tests in test_ablation_runner.py are 4 functions (lines 13/20/32/44), none reference a planted regime.
All 7 ablation settings still report top1=0.0 / top5=0.0 — only the 'random' (=actual) regime ran.
blockers.severity_reason: Honest-marketing absolutism: a [CLAIM] tag in CLAIM.md is a falsifiable assertion. CLAIM.md line 3 itself states 'polyalign asserts this; the assertion is falsifiable by running the cited test'. The cited test does not exercise a planted regime because no planted regime exists in the code. This is the saelet ship-and-yank pattern in miniature — a documented feature with zero implementation backing.
fix_options: (a) Implement the planted regime in scripts/run_ablation.py + add a test in tests/test_ablation_runner.py that exercises it + rerun ablation so results/v0.1.0a1_ablation.json carries `regime` per cell + re-render README.

(b) Downgrade CLAIM.md:45 to [non-CLAIM] with text 'The planted-regime variant of the ablation runner (bundle B = bundle A permuted) is deferred to v0.1.1; v0.1.0a1 ships only the random-decoder regime, which by construction yields top-k ~ 0 (honest disclosure at README:124).' — this is the safer SHIP path.

majors.title: scripts/verify_step.py missing — /compact resume path will cold-restart from S0
majors.evidence_file: scripts/ (no verify_step.py) vs bootstrap_protocol §1 S1 line 99 + §3 line 494
majors.spec: S1 explicitly lists 'scripts/verify_step.py (各 phase 機械検証)' as a required scaffold artifact; §3 line 494: 'verify_step.py 不在 → current_step = "S0" で cold-build'.
majors.impact: Self-resume after /compact will fall back to S0 cold-build, losing protocol state. Not a user-facing ship-blocker; a protocol-fidelity blocker for the self-drive contract.

nits.title: README:75 uses idiom 'first-class SSM carrier support' — borderline on checklist 2
nits.note: 'first-class' is a standard idiom for 'native / fully-supported'; banned-phrases.txt blocks only 'first ever' (CI passes). Recommend rephrase to 'native SSM carrier support' for maximum strictness.

nits.title: neelnanda Crosscoders missing from README prior-art table (checklist 3 lists 6 competitors; README names 5)
nits.fix: Add one bullet differentiating polyalign from neelnanda Crosscoders tutorials.
nits.ci_impact: none — CI grep only enforces gavagai/AlignSAE/crosscoder.

nits.title: [project.scripts] entry-point name drift vs protocol
nits.note: `uv run polyalign-lint --help` rc=0 verified. Entry resolves. Pure protocol-text drift.

nits.title: Word 'novel' used 3x in README/CHANGELOG for polytope-vertex core
nits.note: 'novel' is softer than 'first'; combination (Pareto-front + cycle-consistency + Sinkhorn-OT) is plausibly novel given prior-art table. Defensible. Suggest 'polyalign-specific' or 'newly composed' for maximum strictness.

evidence.finding: [CLAIM] asserts ablation runner ships random + planted regimes with regime key in JSON cell; planted regime returns non-trivial top-k > 0

Only ONE regime implemented: `_make_bundle` builds independent random Gaussian decoders for each bundle. No bundle-B-as-permutation-of-bundle-A code path exists. No `regime` field written to JSON output.
No `regime` key anywhere; `mode: 'synthetic'` is the only mode label.
Four tests: file existence, [DEMO] prefix coverage, JSON existence, determinism. None exercise a planted regime.

prior_art_diff_table.polyalign_diff: pairwise scalar score → polyalign returns ranked top-k vertex set with coverage band + Pareto front (N >= 2, M >= 1)
training-time concept-aligned SAEs → polyalign is post-hoc over pre-trained SAEs
closed 2-model diff → polyalign open-source N-model multi-architecture with OTCP coverage
Euclidean 2-model replication → polyalign adds OTCP + Sinkhorn-OT + SSM carrier
training-time concept-aligned SAEs cross-modal → polyalign post-hoc, no SAE retraining
pedagogical 2-model crosscoder → polyalign N >= 2 multi-architecture with OTCP coverage band — NIT B-N2: add to README:70-76
per-model Matryoshka SAE → polyalign cross-architecture Matryoshka with Sinkhorn-OT pairwise alignment

agent_C.C-B1 (A+B verdicts absent): Agent B's verdict now exists; Agent A still independent.
agent_C.C-B2 (top1/top5 = 0.000 'likely bug' in: Agent B DISAGREES — reading run_ablation.py:95 `target_a = int(row['feature_id_A']) % n_feat` shows the indices ARE remapped into [0, n_feat) range; the zeros are not an off-by-one bug, they are the structurally-expected outcome of random Gaussian decoders against arbitrary synthetic label IDs (no semantic anchor exists). README:124 + CLAIM.md:41 honestly disclose. Recommend Agent C withdraw C-B2 or re-classify as 'no-information ablation' nit. NOTE: this same observation is now CLAIM.md:45's motivation for proposing a planted regime — but that proposal must ship before being CLAIM-tagged (B-B2).
agent_C.C-B3 (CLAIM tag format): RESOLVED by concurrent rewrite. CLAIM.md tags now line-leading (verified: `grep -E '^\[(CLAIM|non-CLAIM)\]' docs/CLAIM.md` yields 23 matches; non-bullet leftovers 0).

verdict_text: DO-NOT-SHIP. One CRITICAL blocker (B-B2): docs/CLAIM.md:45 [CLAIM]-tags a `random`/`planted` two-regime ablation runner that does not exist in code — grep over run_ablation.py, results/v0.1.0a1_ablation.json, and tests/test_ablation_runner.py returns zero hits on `regime` or `planted`. This is a documented-but-unshipped feature behind a [CLAIM] tag, which by CLAIM.md:3's own definition must be falsifiable by running the cited test. Saelet ship-and-yank pattern. Fix by EITHER implementing the planted regime + retest + re-render ablation table OR downgrading line 45 to [non-CLAIM] deferred-to-v0.1.1. One MAJOR (verify_step.py missing) and four NITs unchanged. Agent B confirms the concurrent CLAIM.md rewrite resolved former B-B1 / B-M1 / B-M2. Agent B continues to DISAGREE with Agent C on C-B2 (the zeros are honest disclosure, not a bug).
